event: Treated with noninvasive ventilation for management of ARDS
A large wound developed around the patient's lips and in her mouth 2 d after noninvasive ventilation
The patient was transferred to the gastroenterology department for further management
Laboratory values observed at the time of transfer to gastroenterology
Dermatology was consulted regarding treatment of the patient's perioral wounds
She was discharged from the hospital
The perioral wound progressed to exhibit local bleeding with crusting of blood
Perioral herpes infection secondary to noninvasive ventilation
A gradual communication strategy was used
A nitrofurazone dressing with regular dressing change
Application of topical penciclovir around the lips
Application of mupirocin around the lips
Intramuscular injection of nutritious nerve drugs
Wet application of topical nitrocilin around the lips
After consultation, the cause of the patient's wound was identified as herpes infection
The patient achieved good recovery after multidisciplinary consultation and treatment
The perioral wound measured approximately 10 cm × 10 cm
A gentle dressing change technique was used to prevent injury to the healing skin
Application of topical thrombin lyophilized powder
